title: 2 UP Teens Were Filmed While Bathing In Yamuna. They Consume Poison
content: Superintendent of Police (SP) Arun Kumar Singh said a memo was received from the Pahari community health centre (CHC) stating that two girls, aged 16 and 17, had consumed poison.

title: Arvind Kejriwal Wanted To Pacify AAP MPs, They Quit Before Meeting Him: Sources
content: Before the meeting call with Arvind Kejriwal came, the MPs had not come together as a group to plan their exit, though individually all of them had decided to leave, sources said

title: H-1B Visas To Be Paused For 3 Years? Trump's Aides Introduce New Bill
content: The bill proposes sweeping changes. It would cut the annual H-1B cap from 65,000 to 25,000 and eliminate exemptions. It would replace the lottery system with a wage-based selection process and set a minimum salary of $200,000 per year.

title: 20 Years On, No One On Trial For Malegaon Blasts That Killed 31
content: The state's inability to reconcile the conflicting investigations of the ATS and the NIA has resulted in a collapse of the case.

title: Raghav Chadha's BJP Shocker For Arvind Kejriwal. 6 Other MPs To Switch Too
content: Raghav Chadha to join BJP, says two-thirds of AAP members in Rajya Sabha are merging with the ruling party

title: Woman's Naked Body Found Floating In Ghaziabad Canal
content: The body is said to be in the early stages of decomposition, with officials estimating that the woman died around 8 to 10 days ago.

title: YSRCP MLC Arrested For 'Witness Tampering' In 2022 Driver Murder Case
content: The MLC, accused of the killing, allegedly attempted to tamper with evidence by pressuring key witnesses ahead of the trial proceedings.

title: 'Not An Inch Lost To China During Ladakh Standoff': Ex-Army Chief MM Naravane
content: General Naravane addressed the question of political direction during the standoff, clarifying how decisions flow within the civil-military framework without naming any individual or party.

title: Gurugram Man Invites Fiancee, Takes 'Sex-Enhancing' Drugs, Dies Of Overdose
content: Police suspect that he had taken a sex-enhancing drug, a possible overdose of which may have led to the heart attack.

title: Row After Students Forced To Remove Sacred Thread During Exam In Bengaluru
content: As the controversy took a political turn, the college suspended the invigilator pending inquiry. The government also ordered a probe.

title: "Mamata Banerjee Leaving, BJP Is Coming": Amit Shah On Bengal Polls
content: The first phase of polling, held on Thursday across 152 Assembly constituencies spread over 16 districts, saw turnout reach approximately 92.98 per cent, one of the highest figures in the state's recent electoral history.

title: India Is "Top-Tier Security Partner": Australian Envoy To NDTV
content: The Australian National Defence Strategy places a great deal of importance on India, referring to the South Asian nation as "a top-tier security partner".

title: National Rights Panel Issues Notice Over Explosion At Vedanta Plant Which Killed 25
content: The rights panel has issued notices to the state Chief Secretary and the police chief, calling for a detailed report on the matter within two weeks.

title: US Justice Department Allows Use Of Firing Squads For Federal Executions
content: There were 13 executions by lethal injection during Trump's final six months in power, more than under any US leader in 120 years.

title: Thief Snatches Gold Chain From NRI In Ludhiana, Sister Tries To Fight Him
content: As he attempted to snatch the chain, Raj and her sister immediately fought back and grappled with the robber.

title: In Madhya Pradesh Welfare Schemes, A Tale Of Two Halves
content: The burden of implementing some financial welfare schemes largely rests on public sector banks, which are performing far beyond their targets

title: People Doubted If Ram Temple Will Be Built, But It Happened: RSS Chief
content: Bhagwat was speaking after laying the foundation stone of 'Bharat Durga Shakti Sthal' temple in Nagpur.

title: Economist Ashok Lahiri From West Bengal To Be NITI Aayog Vice-Chairman
content: Scientist Gobardhan Das, also from West Bengal, is set to be named Niti Aayog member, according to a report.

title: Gujarat woman critical after shooting at US store; husband held for ‘accidental’ firing
content: A Gujarat woman's life in Louisiana was shattered by a gunshot during a store negotiation, leaving her critically injured with severe jaw damage. Her husband faces weapon charges as doctors deem her recovery complex, requiring multiple surgeries. A crowdfunding effort is underway to support her extensive medical bills and her dream of a better future.

title: 'Virat Kohli isn't a natural six-hitter but ... ': Kaif after RCB's win over GT
content: Mohammad Kaif praised Virat Kohli for crossing 300 IPL sixes, saying, "Kohli isn't a natural six-hitter but he worked on his game…" highlighting his adaptability. Kohli (81) and Devdutt Padikkal (55) then led RCB’s chase of 206. Kohli said, "His innings was the difference…" as their partnership sealed victory over GT.

title: US sanctions China's 'teapot' refinery & 40 shipping companies over Iran oil trade
content: The US has tightened its grip on Iran's oil revenue by sanctioning a major Chinese refinery and numerous shipping entities involved in transporting Iranian crude. This move targets a "shadow network" and escalates pressure amid regional tensions. China has criticized the sanctions, urging an end to their political use and warning of harm to legitimate trade.

title: What is voluntary retirement programme that Microsoft is offering its 8500-plus employees
content: Microsoft is offering voluntary retirement to approximately 7% of its US workforce, marking its first such program in 51 years. This softer approach, unlike previous layoffs, aims to manage costs and reputation amid stock price pressure and AI infrastructure spending. The offer, based on an age and tenure formula, provides a choice for eligible employees.

title: Mumbai wedding horror: Man dies chasing men who made ‘gestures’ at woman; 10 injured
content: A wedding guest died after falling into a 30-foot septic tank pit in an under-construction building in Bhandup West. The incident occurred when the deceased chased men who allegedly made gestures at a woman. Ten others were injured when a temporary platform they used to rescue the first victim gave way.

title: US justice dept closes criminal investigation into Fed chief Jerome Powell
content: The Justice Department has closed its investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell regarding headquarters renovation cost overruns. This move removes a hurdle for Kevin Warsh's confirmation as Fed chair. The probe's closure allows scrutiny to continue through the Inspector General's office, with potential for criminal investigation restart if warranted.

title: KPMG to lay off 10% of US audit team after failed retirement efforts
content: KPMG is set to cut approximately 10% of its US audit partners after voluntary retirement schemes failed to yield desired results. The accounting giant, one of the Big Four, cited a need to align its partnership size with current business demands.

title: Aamir Khan shares admiration for SRK; keen to work on film with him and Salman Khan
content: Fans' long-held dream of seeing Bollywood's three Khans – Shah Rukh, Salman, and Aamir – together on screen might be closer than ever. Aamir Khan recently expressed his strong desire to collaborate with Shah Rukh, revealing they've discussed it but await the perfect story.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ adds Rs 1.60 cr to tally amid ‘Michael’ and ‘Bhoot Bangla’ buzz
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' continues its impressive box office run, nearing Rs 1,170 crore worldwide and Rs 1,125 crore in India. Despite facing competition from new releases like Hollywood's 'Michael' and Bollywood's 'Bhoot Bangla', the spy drama added Rs 1.60 crore on its 37th day, showcasing its sustained popularity.

title: Starting January 1, Deloitte cuts parental leave, PTO, pension, IVF funding in US
content: Deloitte plans to reduce paid family leave from 16 to eight weeks and cut annual paid time off by 5-10 days for a segment of its US workforce starting January 1, 2027. These changes, affecting internal roles under the 'Center' talent model, aim to tailor benefits to market alignment. New pension plan contributions and adoption/surrogacy reimbursements will also cease.

title: IPL 2026 drama! Suyash taunts Gill after his dismissal - Watch
content: In an IPL 2026 match, Suyash Sharma dismissed Shubman Gill (32) and mocked him by copying his ‘bow down’ celebration, thrilling the Chinnaswamy crowd. Sai Sudharsan smashed a century, helping Gujarat Titans post 205 after a strong opening stand. However, Virat Kohli (81) and Devdutt Padikkal (55) stitched a quick partnership, guiding Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a five-wicket win in 18.5 overs.

title: Men in BMW, Fortuner refuse to pay toll fee, assault and abduct staffer in north Bengaluru
content: Three men in a BMW and Toyota Fortuner allegedly assaulted and briefly abducted a toll plaza employee near Bengaluru after refusing to pay the fee. Claiming to be associates of a local MLA, they forced their way through the barrier, punching and kicking one employee and bundling another into their vehicle.

title: The real wall with ears: Ancient Roman walls can hear you from metres away
content: Ancient Roman structures, utilizing smooth stone and carefully shaped interiors, exhibit remarkable acoustic properties. Curved walls and vast domes guide sound, creating whispering gallery effects where even quiet voices travel with surprising clarity. This architectural design allowed performers in Roman theaters to be heard by large audiences without amplification.

title: Cristiano Ronaldo diet secret: Ex-chef reveals why he avoids milk
content: Cristiano Ronaldo’s former chef has revealed that the forward has completely avoided milk as part of his diet, despite its widespread use in sports nutrition. The insight offers a rare look into the specific choices behind his routine, highlighting a preference for natural foods and strict control over intake, which has supported his longevity at the highest level.‘It’s against nature’: Cristiano Ronaldo’s former chef reveals why he avoids and never drinks milk, shares daily diet details

title: Patrick Mahomes $325 million valuation shock: Off-field move amid recovery
content: Patrick Mahomes, the Kansas City Chiefs’ quarterback, is focused on his recovery from his brutal injuries as his wife, Brittany Mahomes stands by his side through all the difficult times. While both Patrick Mahomes and his wife, Brittany Mahomes, have preferred to be private about the star player’s recovery, the high profile couple recently received an update worth a whopping $325 million.

title: Dianna Russini claim shocks: Alleged confession rocks Mike Vrabel saga
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, has publicly announced that he is taking some time off to seek counselling as the alleged affair rumors with Dianna Russini refuse to die down as new details emerge. Amid all the mess, it seems the situation has taken a turn for the worse as Dianna Russini’s husband and child have been dragged into the drama.

title: Manipur unrest: 3 killed, 5 injured in fresh Naga-Kuki clashes in Ukhrul
content: Two separate gunfights in Manipur's Ukhrul district on Friday morning resulted in three deaths and multiple injuries between rival Naga and Kuki armed groups. A faction of the NSCN also claimed to have killed five Myanmar-based Kuki National Army-Burma cadres in Kamjong district, though this remains unconfirmed.

title: Former MI coach says Hardik Pandya should not be made scapegoat
content: Mumbai were outplayed as Sanju Samson scored an unbeaten 101 off 54 balls, hitting 10 fours and six sixes at a strike rate of 187.04. Samson reached his century on the last ball of the innings with a six as CSK finished on 207/6 in 20 overs.

title: Trump sending Witkoff, Kushner to Pakistan for Iran peace talks, Vance on standby
content: Senior US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are heading to Pakistan for crucial talks with Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi to revive negotiations amid escalating regional tensions. The diplomatic initiative aims to address Tehran's nuclear program and ease global market volatility. Despite overtures, the situation remains volatile with ongoing conflicts impacting vital shipping lanes.

title: 18 balls, zero boundaries: Three overs that cost Gujarat Titans the match vs RCB
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured a crucial victory over Gujarat Titans, chasing down 206 with an 18.5-over finish. Virat Kohli's explosive 81, coupled with Devdutt Padikkal's 55, proved decisive after GT faltered in the death overs, failing to capitalize on a strong start. A dropped catch early on proved costly for the Titans.

title: Bengal polls: Phase-1 turnout is victory of trust over fear, says PM Narendra Modi
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi stated that the record turnout in Bengal's first phase of polls signals the end of Trinamool's "maha jungle raj," equating people's participation to an endorsement of BJP's "bharosa" (trust) over "bhay" (fear).

title: 20 get life over Assam lynching of duo mistaken for child-lifters
content: A Nagaon court has sentenced 20 individuals to life imprisonment for the 2018 lynching of two youths in Assam's Karbi Anglong district. The victims were attacked by a mob on suspicion of being child lifters. The court acquitted 25 other accused due to insufficient evidence.

title: Forget Bengal, we will dethrone BJP from Delhi: CM Mamata Banerjee after phase-1 turnout
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee declared the high phase-one voter turnout a decisive mandate against the BJP, predicting the saffron party would lose significantly. She accused the BJP of atrocities and warned of "democratic revenge" for their actions, urging focus on Manipur's unrest and criticizing their treatment of allies like Nitish Kumar.

title: Babar Azam vs Virat Kohli: 'Hadd se zyada hype, 'king' tag ne chadha diya' - Ex-cricketer's blunt take
content: Former India cricketer Wasim Jaffer has dismissed comparisons between Pakistan's Babar Azam and Virat Kohli. Jaffer feels Azam has received excessive hype. He suggests this has led to pressure on Azam to perform like Kohli. Jaffer also cited captaincy as a factor in Azam's performance dips. Despite this, Jaffer acknowledges Azam's talent and class as one of Pakistan's top batters.

title: South Delhi rape-murder accused’s calm demeanour raises questions
content: Delhi Police plans a psychological evaluation for a man accused of a brutal rape and murder. The former domestic help displayed unsettling calm during interrogation and even checked into a hotel post-crime. Investigators are building a strong case, including recovered evidence and a positive potency test, as relatives are also being questioned.

title: Shocking! New Zealand star cricketer banned for cocaine use
content: Former New Zealand all-rounder Doug Bracewell has received a two-year ban from cricket after testing positive for cocaine during an English County Championship match last year. The 35-year-old admitted to using the substance and has accepted the sanction. This follows a previous suspension for a similar offense in 2024. His ban is backdated to November 2025.

title: Stefon Diggs-Cardi B custody battle? Viral claims denied amid online frenzy
content: Stefon Diggs and Cardi B are trending after viral claims suggested a custody battle and lawsuit between them. The rumors spread quickly online, fueled by an alleged quote and unverified posts. However, there is no confirmed evidence or official statement supporting these claims. For now, the situation remains speculation, highlighting how fast misinformation can circulate on social media.

title: Opposition files new notice seeking CEC Gyanesh Kumar's ouster
content: MPs in Rajya Sabha have submitted a fresh notice to the RS Secretary General seeking to move a motion for removal of CEC Gyanesh Kumar on the ground of “proven misbehaviour”. The opposition has levelled 9 new charges against Kumar in the notice, signed by “73” MPs.

title: India resorts to LPG spot buying to bridge West Asia supply gap
content: India is securing LPG cargo through spot purchases to meet demand for gas cylinders among households as well as commercial establishments. People aware of the development said state-run oil marketing companies have tied up with the US to bridge the supply shortfall.

title: Fans Heartbroken: Final post of triathlete Mara Flavia Araujo after ironman tragedy
content: Brazilian triathlete Mara Flavia Araujo died during the swimming leg of the Ironman Texas triathlon on April 19, 2026. Her last Instagram post, shared hours earlier, has gone viral after her death. Reports from Chosun.com and Yahoo confirm she went missing during the swim. A close friend revealed she was unwell before the race but still chose to compete.

title: 'Dropped on nought': Gill reflects on missed Virat Kohli chance after GT defeat
content: “I mean, when a batsman gets dropped on nought, it's always tough on the fielder. But it's about how you come back in the game. We had our moments. But we weren't able to hit the right lengths consistently, which was important. But they batted very well in the middle overs.”

title: NFL rumor: Packers eye $11M former rams QB Jimmy Garoppolo for veteran upgrade
content: The Green Bay Packers’ performance in the last season dipped since 2024 as the team finished the season with 9 wins and 8 losses, sparking concern among fans about the team. As the Green Bay Packers lost the Wild Card round to the Chicago Bears, all eyes are on the next seasons as the team focuses on its roster for 2026.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for April 25, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for April 25, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: ‘Janivara’ row: BJP demands stern action
content: Ashok alleged that the CET authorities were behaving in an insensitive manner as they were of the view that the present Congress dispensation, led by Chief Minister Siddaramaiah, would be happy if they ask the students remove the ‘janivara’

title: Record turnout in West Bengal poll fuels rival claims, data tempers conclusions
content: Despite 92.88% voting, historical trends show high participation has not consistently translated into anti- or pro-incumbency outcomes

title: Absolute voter increases seen across Tamil Nadu followed spatial patterns
content: Data show the record turnout in the southern State was a consequence of the SIR exercise; while the State also registered an increase in the absolute number of voters who turned out, the provisional addition of roughly 24.8 lakh voters appears lower than the gains registered in recent cycles

title: YSRCP plans to strengthen its farmers’ wing
content: It will be made more proactive to address issues at the grassroots level, says Sajjala Ramakrishna Reddy

title: Economist Ashok Lahiri, scientist Gobardhan Das from Bengal to join Niti Aayog as vice-chairman, member
content: Mr. Lahiri, currently a BJP MLA in West Bengal, is a former chief economic advisor to the government of India and a member of the 15th Finance Commission

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: State may witness close contests in many constituencies
content: The presence of TVK founder Vijay is said to have turned the contest into a three-cornered one instead of the usual bipolar race, paving the way for closer electoral battles than in the past

title: Record turnouts in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu polls a display of citizen power: Supreme Court
content: Solicitor General Tushar Mehta said one ought to focus on the “historic” percentage of polling recorded in both West Bengal and Tamil Nadu

title: Congress leader’s son shot dead, another son injured in Janjgir-Champa in Chhattisgarh
content: Sammelal Kashyap’s elder son Aayush (19) was shot fatally while his younger son Ashutosh (16) suffered a bullet injury to his hand. Police say the attack was carried out by three masked assailants who arrived on a motorcycle

title: 10 of 26 petitioners caught in Russia-Ukraine crossfire dead; some volunteered, others duped, government tells Supreme Court
content: Additional Solicitor General Aishwarya Bhati says 26 petitioners were among 215 individuals who went to Russia.

title: India among countries where Meta ‘automatically’ blocks flagged content
content: India is now part of a small group of countries where content takedown notices are complied with immediately, giving police and the government wide powers of immediate and lasting censorship

title: Madras High Court Madurai Bench orders notice to nine convicts in Sattankulam custodial death case
content: A Division Bench of Justices N. Anand Venkatesh and K.K.  Ramakrishnan ordered notice to the convicts while hearing the referred trial. It posted the matter to April 30 for hearing

title: Birds of Mangalajodi: A visual journey into Chilika’s avian paradise
content: A stunning photographic volume documents Mangalajodi’s vibrant birdlife through years of dedication by a couple whose shared passion transcended loss, blending artistry, patience and conservation into an evocative visual journey celebrating one of the world’s richest wetland habitats

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Post-SIR electorate drop drives turnout surge; votes remain steady compared to 2021 election
content: As of 8 p.m. on polling day, voter turnout stood at 83.41%, significantly higher than 59.75% recorded in 2021 Assembly polls, as per ECI data; this is due to reduction in electorate from 40.57 lakh in 2021 to 28.93 lakh in 2026; number of electors who turned up in both elections remains close to 24 lakh

title: Slender loris electrocuted on power line near Dindigul
content: S. Bharathidasan, secretary of Arulagam, says insulated cables or protective casing on overhead wires must be installed to prevent the deaths of the nocturnal primates

title: Commuters say free rides won’t fix Hyderabad’s MMTS problem
content: While Hyderabad Metro Rail Limited ramps up services during the ongoing TGSRTC strike, MMTS services under South Central Railway remain unchanged, drawing criticism over poor frequency, unreliable schedules and missed opportunities to strengthen one of Hyderabad’s most affordable mass transit networks

title: T.N. records lowest increase in votes polled during Assembly elections over the last 15 years
content: While this election saw an all-time high turnout of 85.15%, a look at the data in the elections held since 2011 shows that there is no uniform pattern between the turnout and electoral verdict

title: Bengaluru firm Shree Bhagavathi Tech Labs to raise ₹50,000 cr to float, fuel 18 startup ventures
content: These ventures, span across diverse domains, are expected to create 18,000 to 20,000 jobs in the City in five years

title: SC/ST Commission recommends action against officials for non-removal of caste from name of ward in Srivilliputhur constituency
content: Residents of the area boycotted the election to protest the government’s failure to remove the caste name

title: Congress leaders introspect on not aligning with TVK
content: Many have said Congress leader Rahul Gandhi, who did not share the stage with Chief Minister M.K. Stalin and was widely believed to have favoured an alliance with the TVK, had assessed the situation accurately when he saw an opportunity to rejuvenate the Congress in Tamil Nadu

title: IREL seeks CRZ clearance for atomic minerals project in Kanniyakumari district
content: The locations identified include Keezhmidalam, Midalam, Enayamputhenthurai, Ezhudesam, and Kollengode

title: Polling percentage of women rises in T.N. this Assembly election
content: As per provisional figures available at 8 p.m. on Friday, two Assembly constituencies in Tamil Nadu — Karur and Veerapandi — posted 93.40% each, the highest polling percentage among the Assembly segments that went to polls on April 23

title: Allahabad HC raps U.P. police for ‘disturbing trend’ of harassing young couples
content: The bench directed the State DGP to take corrective steps to address practices where police chase young couples who have married out of their own will

title: Mundathikode fireworks accident: The spark that turned fatal
content: On April 21, a deadly explosion at a fireworks unit at Mundathikode claimed at least 12 lives, injured more than 24, and damaged at least 50 houses nearby. The unit was set amid vast paddy fields and housed multiple sheds storing large quantities of explosive materials. Investigations are under way. Mini Muringatheri and Aathira Haridas report on the latest tragedy in a hazardous trade where violations are common

title: Denial of bail to MP Rashid to meet ailing father ‘nothing short of inhuman’, says brother
content: “This is not just about one individual, but the story of hundreds of Kashmiris in jails, and it is about the conscience of a system that must answer for its fairness and humanity”

title: VVITU hosts seminar on intellectual property rights
content: The programme aims at underlining the role of intelluctual property rights in encouraging original thinking while safeguarding intellectual output

title: SRM University-A.P. celebrates 10th edition of Research Day
content: Research Day event receives 574 research abstracts spanning fields such as artificial intelligence, renewable energy and robotics

title: Urdu no longer mandatory for J&K revenue services; sparks row
content: the move comes nearly a year after BJP protested and sought revocation of a govt order that made Urdu a compulsory subject for the naib tehsildar recruitment exam in J&K . BJP called it a deliberate attempt to sideline and erase Urdu from the region’s administrative framework .

title: Army chief inducted into International Hall of Fame at US Army War College
content: "General Upendra Dwivedi, COAS, visited the Army War College, Carlisle Barracks, USA, where he was inducted into the international Hall of Fame . he also addressed the faculty and international student officers' leadership, professional military education and evolving security dynamics .

title: High voter turnout in Bengal and Tamil Nadu true signs of democracy, says Supreme Court
content: suprem court hails record 92% voter turnout in first phase of west Bengal assembly elections . a bench of justices made the remark after a vociferous opponent of SIR said, "One good thing happened. Record 92% voting happened because all migrant labourers rushed to the state to cast their vote in the fear that if they didn't, their names would be struck off the electoral roll"

title: Develop space toilet, world needs it: Indian astronaut Prashant Nair asks desi startups
content: astronaut-designate group captain Prashant Nair is being trained for the gaganyaan mission along with Grp Capt Shubhanshu Shukla . he has requested Indian startups to make space toilet as the world needs more research on that front .

title: Calcutta HC changes EC’s 48-hour ban order on bike movement to 12 hours
content: pillion riding would not be allowed on two-wheelers for 12 hours on poll day unless it was for taking or bringing back children from school, medical emergencies or family functions . the court, however, did not interfere with the 48-hour ban on motorcycle rallies .

title: Ex-CEA & BJP leader Ashok Lahiri to be Niti Aayog vice-chairman
content: former chief economic adviser is to be nominated as a member of the govt’s premier think tank . the two appointments come amid polls in west Bengal with the second round of voting on Wednesday and will be seen as an overture to the electorate .

title: With no repolling recommendations, Bengal & Tamil Nadu break 'past pattern', says EC
content: Bharti Jain is senior editor with The Times of India, New Delhi. She has been writing on security matters since 1996. Having covered the Union home ministry, security agencies, Election Commission and the ‘prime’ political beat, the Congress, for The Economic Times all these years, she moved to TOI in August 2012. Her repertoire of news stories delves into the whole gamut of issues related to terrorism and internal strife, besides probing strategic affairs in India’s neighbourhood. Read More

title: 'No meeting planned': Iran rules out direct talks with US
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

content: Determine the monthly installment amount for a loan Estimate the returns on investments made through SIPs Find out maturity amount and interest earned on PPF Check maturity amount and interest earned on an FD Estimate the pension amount and corpus accumulated under NPS A Mutual Fund Calculator helps estimate the future value of investments

title: Iran foreign minister reaches Pakistan; US to send Kushner, Witkoff for talks
content: envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan, the white house says . the trip comes as much of the world is on edge over a war that has snarled crucial energy exports through the Strait of Hormuz, clouded the global economic picture .

title: Why anti-defection law won't apply to Raghav Chadha, 6 AAP MPs
content: legal experts highlight the inability to stop political defections when they acquire the scale of 'merger' the law approves two-thirds of a legislature party to break away from the party on whose ticket they got elected and merged with another party . senior advocates say the penalty of disqualification may not arise .

title: Rajya Sabha numbers game: AAP exodus pushes NDA 17 seats past majority
content: seven of the 10 Rajya Sabha MPs of AAP, including Raghav Chadha and ex-international cricketer Harbhajan Singh, joined BJP on friday . this comes at a time when former CM Arvind Kejriwal has intensified his campaign against BJP after his discharge in the excise policy case .

title: Rajnath visits German submarine yard as P-75I deal is expected soon
content: defence minister was briefed on advanced naval technologies and capabilities . he was accompanied by german defence minister Boris Pistorius . the visit comes as India moves closer to finalising its project-75 India programme . six advanced submarines will be built at Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited .
